metadata.notes: agent=yes daemon=full driver=gap — three teardown variants observable via process-exit sweep; the scaffold driver stubs wait_turn/exit_clean/restart/sigkill.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

**agent=yes.** `agy` is a standalone native binary (1 process ↔ 1 conversation) that can exit cleanly (Ctrl-D / Ctrl-C) or be killed. All three session-end variants (clean exit after a no-op turn, SIGKILL mid-idle, SIGKILL mid-turn) are achievable.

**daemon=full.** The ExactName{agy} matcher (`adapter.go:36`) binds the live process, and the process-lifecycle observer surfaces process_exited on death. Because `agy` is a single native process per conversation (no Node launcher/worker split like gemini-cli), the death is a clean single-PID exit — the over-asserted multi-process race that made gemini-cli's clean-exit variant emit no process_exited does not apply here. The session settles to ready before/at exit and disappears within readyTTL.

**driver=gap.** The scaffold `driver-interactive.sh` stubs `wait_turn`, `exit_clean`, `restart`, and `sigkill` as `not_implemented` (DRIVE_ELICITS = send/slash/sleep only). `record` must port all four (plus the multi-session session.uuids/transcript.paths epilogue — three variants = three sessions) from a reference driver before driving.
caveats: Variant 3 (SIGKILL mid-turn): the post-crash sweep-to-ready transition may not land inside the recording's tail window — the live dashboard handles it via the process-exit sweep, but a short tail can end with the session still in working. The spec anchors v3 without requiring an explicit post-crash ready phase (mirrors the gemini-cli sibling).

Antigravity emits no explicit end-of-turn marker; turn_done is inferred from a terminal MODEL/PLANNER_RESPONSE with no tool_calls. daemon=full is provisional on what agy writes mid-turn — a live recording in record settles it.

Whether agy's clean Ctrl-D exit reliably emits process_exited in-window is provisional (single-process, so more likely than gemini-cli's launcher race, but unconfirmed); record promotes it from provisional to settled.
Driver gap is the recording blocker: wait_turn/exit_clean/restart/sigkill are not_implemented stubs.

sources.note: ExactName{agy} matcher binds the CLI process for liveness
DiscoverPID — single native process per conversation, no argv filtering needed
sibling presession session-end spec template (3-variant arc)

preconditions: agy CLI on PATH (1.0.10), Google OAuth authenticated; no API key
irrlichd recording — run-cell.sh isolated mode, or --attach against a daemon started with --record
DRIVER GAP: wait_turn, exit_clean, restart, sigkill are not_implemented in driver-interactive.sh — record must port them (and the multi-session epilogue) before driving
setup: Driver creates 3 fresh cwds under <staging>/cwd, cwd-2, cwd-3 (one per variant)
Launch each session with agy --dangerously-skip-permissions for unattended driving

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok
Reply with exactly the word: ok
Write a 200 word essay about the history of coffee.

verify: events.jsonl contains 3 distinct conv-id session_ids plus their proc-<PID> presession rows
variant 1: clean Ctrl-D exit after the turn — session in ready immediately preceding its process_exited
variant 2: SIGKILL mid-idle — process_exited fires while the session is in ready; no transition to working/waiting after
variant 3: SIGKILL mid-turn — session is in working when killed; the sweep-to-ready may or may not land in the 8s tail (accept either)
the state band shows three colored arcs separated by grey gaps
